human_actions_pending.action: Decide whether to add attendee_summary field to calendar-read output
human_actions_pending.context: attendeeCountText is already parsed (e.g. 'Accepted 5, Didn't respond 3') but not returned. User asked if we can get more data besides meeting_link — agent explained this and offered to add it.

decisions.decision: No auto-fetch of Teams meeting link via View event click
decisions.rationale: User: 'I also recommend that we don't automatically try to pull teams links if it requires querying every single meeting'. is_online_meeting: true is the reliable signal instead.

decisions.decision: Week/day view is correct for calendar listing; search-based listing for time periods not available
decisions.rationale: User confirmed: 'the calendar search/query doesn't appear to be an option, so I think this is the only real way to do this'

decisions.decision: meeting_link: null documented as expected behavior for Teams meetings
decisions.rationale: Outlook popup card shows truncated body; full Teams invite HTML (with anchor) only loads when View event is opened. is_online_meeting: true is the authoritative Teams meeting signal.

next_action: Commit the pending changes to lib/calendar.js (removed fetchMeetingLinkViaFullEvent, updated meeting_link comment) and references/calendar-events.md (updated meeting_link field description). Then address the user's attendee_summary question, then run remaining UAT tests.
context_notes: Phase 6 all 4 plans executed and all code verified. We are in the UAT/verification phase. Test 1 passed. Test 2 (calendar-read) was run and led to a Teams link investigation — now resolved by documenting the limitation. Tests 2 re-run and Test 3 (calendar-search) are the remaining live checks before phase approval. User asked about getting more data from calendar-read; agent responded that attendeeCountText is captured but not returned (quick win). Awaiting user decision on that.
